findings.claim_text: Production deployment is targeted for the Rubin Ultra "Kyber" rack in 2027, with 100 kW–1 MW per rack as the design envelope.
NVIDIA claims ~5% better end-to-end efficiency, ~45% less rack copper, and ~30% lower TCO versus the 54 V baseline.

The OCP Diablo 400 specification (codename "Mt. Diablo"), authored by named engineers from Meta and Google, defines a three-wire bus: +400 V, −400 V, and a common/midpoint return — a bipolar topology that yields 800 V differential between the rails. The spec also documents a two-wire 800 V monopolar design option at the rectifier output.
